tier-1.description: Static validation scripts — no LLM, <10s, free

scripts.name: validate-skill-structure
scripts.checks: Every SKILL.md has name, description, inputs, outputs, chain in frontmatter. Pipeline Continuation section (except workflow-compass, framework-test). Audit Mode section for the 11 skills that have it.

scripts.name: validate-contracts
scripts.checks: Input paths use valid docs/specs/ patterns. Output paths don't conflict across skills. Status transitions are valid lifecycle values.

scripts.name: validate-chain-references
scripts.checks: Every chain.lanes.*.next and *.prev references a real skill in includedSkills. Lane skill lists in manifest match chain positions in individual SKILL.md frontmatter.

scripts.name: validate-self-verify-sections
scripts.checks: Every skill with self_verify: true has a Self-Verify table with PASS/FAIL column.

scripts.name: validate-worktree-safety
scripts.checks: .gitignore has .worktrees/, scripts/worktree.sh exists, no orphaned worktrees.

tier-1.5.description: Skill comprehension and triggering tests — LLM-powered, ~5K tokens each, ~30-60s

tests.name: skill-comprehension
tests.checks: Asks Claude factual questions about skill rules (chain order, lifecycle states, TDD enforcement, self-verify gating). Verifies answers contain expected patterns.

tests.name: skill-triggering
tests.checks: Sends naive user prompts (no skill name mentioned) and verifies correct skill is triggered via stream-json Skill tool invocation. Also checks for premature tool use before skill loading.

tier-2.description: Integration scenarios via claude -p — ~50K tokens each

judge_prompts.name: completeness
judge_prompts.scores: 0-10: does skill output address every input requirement?

judge_prompts.name: consistency
judge_prompts.scores: 0-10: does output contradict anything from prior phases?

judge_prompts.name: actionability
judge_prompts.scores: 0-10: can the next skill in the chain use this output?

evals.prompt: Run the pipeline integrity validation on the todo-api example
evals.expected_output: All checks pass: vision, personas, feature specs (Feature + Enabler types), journeys with AC cross-references, UX/UI designs, design system, manifest

assertions.description: At least one PASS in output

evals.prompt: Generate a health check endpoint 3 times with no vibomatic phases (just a one-line prompt) and 3 times with full pipeline (spec → tech design → code). Measure pairwise diff variance between the 3 outputs in each group.
evals.expected_output: Full-pipeline group has lower pairwise diff variance than no-phases group, demonstrating that progressive narrowing reduces implementation variance

evals.prompt: Take the todo-api feature spec and plant 5 known errors (contradicting AC, nonexistent file reference, ungrounded precondition, compound story, untestable AC). Run single-pass review and full review protocol. Count how many errors each approach finds.
evals.expected_output: Full review protocol finds more planted errors than single-pass review

assertions.description: Protocol finds at least as many errors
Protocol finds at least 4 of 5 planted errors
